---
title: "Data types HW 1"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-09"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

Let's talk about our data types

Character - This is text we create it with quotations "text example"

rm(list = ls())


```{r}
install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
```



```{r}
characterex <- "my name"

characterex2 <- c("5")

characterex3 <- c(5)

multipliedcharacterex3 <- 5*characterex3

multipliedcharacterex3

#multipliedcharacterex2 <- 5*characterex2

str(characterex2)
str(characterex3)

```

print(characterex)

Factor data 
```{r}
factordata <- c("sophmore","freshman")

factordata <- as.factor(factordata)

summary(factordata)

```


Integers - numbers that are 

```{r}
intershere <- c(1,3,5,6)


intershere <- as.integer(intershere)  

```


Numeric data - this is everything including integers that is also rational and non chr

```{r}
numberline <- c(1.5,6.3,93.8)

numbersasints <- as.integer(numberline)
```

Logical values - these are true or false


```{r}
Isthisaninteger <- is.integer(characterex)

Isthisaninteger

is.integer(numbersasints)
```

print(HW1 vector)

the data type is numeric. 

## Displacement (cc) Net Power Out (kW) Net Torque (Nm)
## 163.00 3.60 10.30
## Oil Capacity (l) Fuel Tank Capacity (l)
## 0.58 3.10

Make a vector with the above info


```{r}
### We assign the appropriate numeric data to HW1vector
HW1Vector <- c(163.00, 3.60, 10.3,.58,3.10)

### This code names the vector

names(HW1Vector) <- c("Displacement (cc)","Net Power Out (kW)","Net Torque (Nm)","Oil Capacity (L)", "Fuel Tank Capacity (l)")

str(HW1Vector)
#### Validation the object is a vector

is.vector(HW1Vector)
## We validate the work here
print(HW1Vector)

Engine1 <- HW1Vector
```
partB
The goal of this chunk is to write a second vector of character data

```{r}
#Engine2 <- as.character(Engine1)


#Engine2 <- c("163.00","3.60","10.3",".58","3.1")
names(Engine2)  <- c("Displacement (cc)","Net Power Out (kW)","Net Torque (Nm)","Oil Capacity (L)", "Fuel Tank Capacity (l)")

Engine2
```
Family Question
```{r}
FamMembers <- c("Will ","Bill")
names(FamMembers) <- c("Self","Dad")
###Data validation check
FamMembers
str(FamMembers)

```



Question 2
Matrix operations

```{r}
#composing Matrix data
matinfo <- c("AA", -3.3,8.2,  "BB",-1.0,10.5 ,"CC",1.3,12.8)


# function to create matrix
HW1Matrix <- matrix(data = matinfo, ncol = 3 )

#validate

is.matrix(HW1Matrix)
```
```{r}
row1 <- c("AA", "BB", "CC", "DD", "EE")
row2 <- c(-3.3, -1.0, 1.3, 3.6, 5.9)
row3 <- c( 8.2, 10.5, 12.8,15.1, 17.4)

NewMatrix <- rbind(row1,row2,row3)

#HW1Matrix <- rbind(HW1Matrix,row1,row2,row3)

```


## AA BB CC DD EE
## [1,] -3.3 -1.0 1.3 3.6 5.9
## [2,] 8.2 10.5 12.8 15.1 17.4

```{r}
#MatrixMathex1 <- NewMatrix*3

newrow2 <- 3+row2
NewMatrix <- rbind(row1,newrow2,row3)
NewMatrix[2,]

```

```{r}


MultipliedMatrix <- rbind(row1, 3*as.numeric(NewMatrix[2,]),row3)

```





```{r}

matdata <- c("AA",-3.3,8.2,"bb",-1,10.5,"cc",1.3,12.8)
matex2 <- matrix(data = matdata, nrow = 3, ncol = 3)


```









```{r}
E1values <- c(163,3.6,10.30,.58,3.10)
E1datalabels <- c("Displacement","Net Power Out","Net Torque","Oil Cap","Fuel Tank")
#labels()
is.vector(E1values)
names(E1values) <- E1datalabels

attributes(E1values)
str(E1values)
dim(E1values)
nrow(E1values)
length(E1values)
```

```{r}
multe1 <- 3*E1values
```





```{r}
hwcol <- c("AA","BB","CC")
hwrow1 <- c(-3.3,-1.0,1.3)
hwrow2 <- c(8.2,10.5,12.8)


combinedv <- c(hwcol,hwrow1,hwrow2)
HW1mat <- matrix(data = combinedv, ncol =3)
str(HW1mat)
```
```{r}
bettermat <- rbind(hwcol,hwrow1,hwrow2)
```


```{r}
specificvalue <- bettermat[3,2]
```
